patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "Hi there. It's Doctor Smith from Babylon. Can you confirm your name, date of birth, and your home address, please?"] Um, it's <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>. <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>. Um, I can't hear you at the moment actually. Could you turn it up, the, volume up?"] Uh, can you hear me better now?
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>. Yeah, that's better. Thank you."] Um, OK. Sorry about that.
[Clinician asked: "Are you in a private place you can have a consultation?"] Uh, yes.

[Clinician asked: "What can I do for you today?"] Um, I've been having this, blinding headache for a few days. Um, and it's like, really bothering me and , I find it very hard to focus or, like I can't even go to work.

[Clinician asked: "OK, and any headaches in the past before this?"] Not really just they're, on and off. Um, I recently had a, a one for like two days, um, but it just went away with Ibuprofen, so nothing like, more, longer than two days before.

[Clinician asked: "So, how did this start?"] Um, it just started all of a sudden. I woke up one morning with a headache, and that was three days ago, and I just keep having it. Doesn't really go away with Ibuprofen either, I try that, but it just. It gets a little bit better but like, doesn't go away.
[Clinician asked: "OK, and whereabouts in your head is the pain?"] Um, it's kind of all over but, I feel like over my right eye, it's um bit, stronger.

[Clinician asked: "OK. Any problems with your vision? Or, any problems tolerating the light?"] Um, lights definitely make me more sensitive. Like, um, I don't really, like strong lights. Um, and um, yeah, vision I think, I get this, shooting light sometimes. Um, not sure how to explain it. Just a bit of um, weird kind of vision.
[Clinician asked: "OK, and um, any, any nausea or vomiting?"] Uh, no, feeling a bit sick, but no vomiting.
[Clinician asked: "Any tingling? Or numbness? Or weakness anywhere?"] Um, no.
[Clinician asked: "Any change in your smell or taste?"] No, none at all.

[Clinician asked: "Um, and, in the past, when you've had headaches, what have they been like?"] Um, I was sick but I had no more headache, it's just um, a bit of a pressure, in between my eyes, um. But nothing major.

[Clinician asked: "OK, and, with, with this, has it been there all the time? Or is it coming and going?"] Kinda all the time. Um, so when I take Ibuprofen it gets a bit better, so it's a bit, like less um, it, it bothered me less, but then I still have it, it's still there.

[Clinician asked: "Um, and what would you say, the severity is? If, if ten is the worst pain you've ever had, and zero is nothing, what would you say the pain is like?"] I guess it's mostly, eight, and then it kinda comes down to maybe a five.
[Clinician asked: "OK, and what about at night-time, are you able to fall asleep?"] Um, yeah, I'm, sleeping well.
[Clinician asked: "Is it waking you up in the night-time?"] No, no, no. But I can wake up with it in the morning.

[Clinician asked: "OK. Any worse when you change position, or bend over, or cough, or anything like that?"] Mmm. Sometimes when I stand up, quickly I get a bit of a, stabbing pain. Um, or if I throw my head like, it I kind of feel like something is moving inside.
[Clinician asked: "OK. And have you had any, uh, eye checks recently?"] Um, yeah, actually had one recently and, nothing changed my vision.
[Clinician asked: "OK. And have you had any cold symptoms, sore throat, sore ears, or pain over the sinuses? Any cough or flem?"] No, no. <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>
[Clinician asked: "No. Any, any nose bleeds, or coughing up any blood or anything?"] No.

[Clinician asked: "And just recently, have you been well, or have you had any general problems? Any change in your weight? Or, tiredness, lack of energy, anything like that?"] Um, no change of weight, maybe um, lack of energy but it might be because, I'm just having these headaches. so I'm find it quite difficult to go through my daily activities , you know when you have this constant headache.
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> Have you been at work recently?"] Um, I work from home but, couldn't really manage to, do it all the time. So sometimes I just took a day off.

[Clinician asked: "So, you've been, you've had a headache for three days. , how many days have you missed off work?"] Um, so I worked from home all these three days and, um. First day I worked from home and then, um, the second I just took the whole day off. And, today I just tried to work from home the first half of the day but, I, I just stopped in the morning 'cause, I couldn't focus.

[Clinician asked: "OK. And, any other, medical problems in the past?"] Um, I think I only had a, a  pain, um, when I was younger but, no causes found and that went away, but that's, the only thing I know of.
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> OK. Have you been abroad recently at all?"] No.
[Clinician asked: "Do you take any medications?"] Um, just the pill.
[Clinician asked: "Which one?"] Um, .
[Clinician asked: "And when did you start that?"] Um, about a year ago.
[Clinician asked: "Any side effects with that?"] Uh, no I don't think so.

[Clinician asked: "Have you noticed any, any um, increased frequency of headaches since you started that?"] Um, no, <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>, these headaches just very recently, but nothing um, <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> but nothing really changes.

[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> OK. And when you'd , when you've had, when you described your headaches in the past, you said they were just general. Did you have any visual symptoms? Flashing lights? Nausea, vomiting with them?"] No. No, no. No nothing, none of those.
[Clinician asked: "Have you had your blood pressure checked recently with your pill?"] Um, yeah, I had it checked in the pharmacy when I got the pill, and they say it was normal.
[Clinician asked: "And when was that?"] Uh, a year ago.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Um, and any allergies to anything?"] No.
[Clinician asked: "And who do you live with at home?"] Sorry?
[Clinician asked: "Who do you live with at home?"] Uh, just by myself.
[Clinician asked: "OK. And do you smoke?"] Uh, no.
[Clinician asked: "Do you drink any alcohol regularly?"] Yeah.
[Clinician asked: "How much in a normal week do you think?"] Um, probably like, three , once a week.
[Clinician asked: "<INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>. OK. And do you take any other drugs at all?"] Um, no apart from the Ibuprofen that I take, sometimes when, the headache  gets really bad, nothing else.
[Clinician asked: "OK. Um. So, uh, I mean it's obviously the first time you've had such a bad headache. And you said you'd had some altered light in front of your eyes as well?"] Yeah.
[Clinician asked: "But you haven't had a high temperature. Have you checked your temperature?"] Uh, no I didn't, I don't really feel, feverish or anything.

[Clinician asked: "OK. Um, I mean it, it, it could possibly be a migraine type headache. But, the fact it's still ongoing for three days, we probably should, book you into an appointment to get an examination."] <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/>
[Clinician asked: "Um, obviously , if it settles, in the meantime then, we can cancel that. But we should do a neurological examination, look at the back of your eyes as well."] OK.

[Clinician asked: "Um, in terms of medication, I can prescribe you something, similar to Ibuprofen, but a bit stronger which you can take instead of Ibuprofen. And you can also take Paracetamol at the same time."] <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> OK.

[Clinician asked: "So if you're happy to book a, , appointment with us, with the support line, for us to do a full examination. And start, the medication which I prescribe to you, take it with food. If, if you're, you said that when you go to bed your headache's, OK, and not waking you up in the night-time."] Yeah it's not, um but, you mentioned migraine and that um, reminded that, my mum, suffers from, like she has migraines, um.

[Clinician asked: "OK. Any other family history of medical problems?"] Um, no I think that's it, but I know that my mum like, she has quite bad migraines, she's like, has the vomiting and everything, .

[Clinician asked: "OK that's fine. Um, I mean if, if in the meantime before your appointment your, headache is getting unbearable, getting worse or changing. Then , um, if it's that bad, then you should go to A and E and have an examination. Um, , you can call us for further advice on that."] OK. Mm-hmm, OK.

[Clinician asked: "And when, when you have a, an examination with us, we'll check your blood pressure as well. Just to, make sure your pill's still appropriate for you, because sometimes if we, if we do think it's a migraine, then you would have to change your pill. If you've had these flashing lights, and things like"] <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> OK, . Is there anything in the pill that can cause it? Or is it?

[Clinician asked: "Well , some , so that pill if you were having , regular migraines with um, flashing lights, or , other symptoms. It wouldn't be suitable to stay on that pill, particularly, we'd have to change it to something else."] All right.
[Clinician asked: "But you can carry on with that for the moment."] OK.
[Clinician asked: "Have you got any other questions?"] Um, so will you like, um, send me a referral to a specialist, or do I have to <INAUDIBLE_SPEECH/> to confirm?
[Clinician asked: "Uh. Yeah, if you book an appointment with the support team, then we can do a full examination then."] OK. Perfect.
[Clinician asked: "All right then. Take care."] Thank you very much.
gold_note:
PC : Headache

HPC: 3/7 hx headache. Starts on waking up. Over right eye. Seeing lights in front of eyes. Can be worse when stands up not when bends over/cough/sneeze. Vision not disturbed. No nausea/vomiting. No altered taste/smell. Worse in the light. No rash. No fever. No coryzal symptoms. Not woken up in the night. No tingling/numbness/weakness. 
Well before this episode. Has been lacking in energy but no wt loss. 
Severity 5-8/10. Taking ibuprofen, eases slightly but not resolving. 

Has had some generalised headaches before but not bad and associated symptoms/altered vision.
PMH: Nil
DH: Mercilon. BP last checked 1 yr ago was normal.

FMHx: Mum has migraine.

SH: Non smoker. No illicit drugs, 3 beers/week. Works from home.

Imp: Likely migraine but as ongoing and persistent needs neurological examination to exclude other pathology. Advised regular paracetamol and naproxen with food (will prescribe). If gets worse in meantime contact us straightaway or go to A&E is severe/unrelenting.

gold_sections.HIGHLIGHTS:
Starts on waking up
Over right eye
Seeing lights in front of eyes
Worse in the light
No rash
No fever
Not woken up in the night

naive.response_text:
I'm really sorry to hear about the severe headache you've been experiencing. It sounds like it's been quite distressing and is impacting your daily life, especially your ability to work. I understand how frustrating and debilitating this must be.

Based on your description, we should schedule a neurological examination to ensure everything is checked thoroughly. In the meantime, I can prescribe a stronger pain reliever to help manage the pain, which you can take alongside paracetamol if needed. Please make sure to take it with food to avoid any stomach issues.

Given your family history of migraines and the symptoms you're describing, such as sensitivity to light and visual disturbances, we'll also be considering the possibility of migraines. However, it's important to confirm this through a proper examination. 

If your symptoms worsen or change before your appointment, don't hesitate to seek immediate care at the emergency room. We'll also check your blood pressure during your exam to ensure your current medication is still suitable for you.

Please let us know if you have any further questions or concerns. We're here to support you through this. Take care and I hope you start feeling better soon.

strong_prompt.response_text:
Thank you for sharing your concerns with me. It sounds like this headache has been quite distressing, especially with how it's affecting your ability to work and focus. I can understand how frustrating and overwhelming this must be, and I'm here to help you navigate through it.

Given the details you've provided—such as the sudden onset, the persistence of the pain despite ibuprofen, sensitivity to light, and the visual disturbances—it's important to consider several possibilities. Migraines are a strong consideration, especially since they can run in families, as your mother also experiences them. However, it's equally important to rule out other causes, such as tension headaches or even secondary headaches, though less likely given your symptoms.

To move forward, I recommend scheduling an appointment for a thorough neurological examination. This will allow us to assess your condition more closely and determine the best course of action. In the meantime, I can prescribe a stronger pain reliever to help manage the discomfort. It's also crucial to monitor your blood pressure, as we discussed, to ensure your current medication remains suitable.

If your symptoms worsen or if you experience any new signs like severe dizziness, vision changes, or difficulty speaking, please seek immediate care at the ER. Otherwise, let's plan to follow up within a week to review how you're feeling and adjust our approach as needed.

Before we conclude, could you summarize the plan in your own words to ensure everything is clear? This will help confirm that we're aligned on the next steps.

It sounds like you're really struggling with these headaches. They've been going on for three days, and you're finding it hard to focus and even work from home. I can hear how much this is affecting your daily life.

I want to make sure I explained this clearly — can you tell me, in your own words, what we're going to do next and why?

Let's plan to see each other again in a few days. If your headaches get worse or you develop new symptoms like severe pain, vision changes, or nausea, please call us right away.

full.response_text:
I can hear how frustrating this must be for you. You mentioned, "I find it very hard to focus or, like I can't even go to work," and I want to acknowledge how challenging that must be.

The severity and duration of your headache, along with the associated symptoms like difficulty focusing and light sensitivity, suggest we should consider several possible causes. We'll start with some basic tests to rule out common issues and then move to more specific evaluations if needed.

We'll begin with a comprehensive neurological evaluation to assess the possible causes of your headaches.

Depending on the initial findings, we may recommend imaging studies or blood tests to rule out any underlying conditions.

We'll also schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to review the results and discuss the next steps together.
